title: RevolveFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a revolve feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setAngleExtent
methods.description: Defines the extent of the revolution to be at a specified angle. An angle and whether the extent is symmetric or only in one direction is specified. If it's not symmetric a positive or negative angle can be used to control the direction. If symmetric, the angle is the angle on one side so the entire angle of the revolution will be twice the specified angle. Use an angle of 360 deg or 2 pi radians to create a full revolve.

methods.name: setOneSideToExtent
methods.description: Defines the extent of the revolve to be from the sketch or profile plane to the specified "To" face.

methods.name: setTwoSideAngleExtent
methods.description: Defines the angle of the revolve to be to applied to both sides of the profile at the specified angles.

methods.name: setTwoSideToExtent
methods.description: Defines the extents of the revolve to be from the sketch plane to specified faces in both directions. If the matchShape argument is true, the faces to revolve to are extended to fully intersect the revolve.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Gets and sets the entity used to define the axis of revolution. The axis can be a sketch line, construction axis, linear edge or a face that defines an axis (cylinder, cone, torus, etc.). If it is not in the same plane as the profile, it is projected onto the profile plane.

properties.name: creationOccurrence
properties.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the Revolve is created based on geometry (e.g. a profile and/or face(s)) in another component AND (the Revolve) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI

properties.name: isProjectAxis
properties.description: Specifies if the axis should be projected on the same plane as the profile sketch plane or not.   Setting this to true will use a projected axis, while setting it to false will keep it in its original location. This is initialized to false so the selected axis will be used in the feature.

properties.name: isSolid
properties.description: Specifies if the revolution should be created as a solid or surface. If it's a surface then there aren't any end caps and it's open. This is initialized to true so a solid will be created if it's not changed.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: operation
properties.description: Gets and sets the type of operation performed by the revolve.

properties.name: participantBodies
properties.description: Gets and sets the list of bodies that will participate in the feature when the operation is a cut or intersection.   If this property has not been set, the default behavior is that all bodies that are intersected by the feature will participate.   This property can return null in the case where the feature has not been fully defined so that possible intersecting bodies can be computed.

properties.name: profile
properties.description: Gets and sets the profiles or planar faces used to define the shape of the revolve. This property can return or be set with a single Profile, a single planar face, or an ObjectCollection consisting of multiple profiles and planar faces. When an ObjectCollection is used all of the profiles and faces must be co-planar. This property returns null in the case where the feature is non-parametric.   To create a surface (non-solid) revolution, you can use the createOpenProfile and createBRepEdgeProfile methods of the Component object to create an open profile. The isSolid property of the RevolveFeatureInput property must also be False.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

axis.title: RevolveFeatureInput.axis Property
axis.description: Gets and sets the entity used to define the axis of revolution. The axis can be a sketch line, construction axis, linear edge or a face that defines an axis (cylinder, cone, torus, etc.). If it is not in the same plane as the profile, it is projected onto the profile plane.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = revolveFeatureInput_var.axis

# Set the value of the property.
revolveFeatureInput_var.axis = propertyValue

classType.title: RevolveFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setAngleExtent.title: RevolveFeatureInput.setAngleExtent Method
setAngleExtent.description: Defines the extent of the revolution to be at a specified angle. An angle and whether the extent is symmetric or only in one direction is specified. If it's not symmetric a positive or negative angle can be used to control the direction. If symmetric, the angle is the angle on one side so the entire angle of the revolution will be twice the specified angle. Use an angle of 360 deg or 2 pi radians to create a full revolve.

parameters.name: isSymmetric
parameters.description: Set to 'true' for a revolve symmetrical about the profile plane

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle of the revolution

related_samples.name: revolveFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a revolve feature using an angle extent.

related_samples.name: Simple Revolve Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new revolve feature, resulting in a new component.

setOneSideToExtent.title: RevolveFeatureInput.setOneSideToExtent Method
setOneSideToExtent.description: Defines the extent of the revolve to be from the sketch or profile plane to the specified "To" face.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = revolveFeatureInput_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = revolveFeatureInput_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity, directionHint)

parameters.name: toEntity
parameters.description: The entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.

parameters.name: directionHint
parameters.description: Specifies the direction of the revolve.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setTwoSideAngleExtent.title: RevolveFeatureInput.setTwoSideAngleExtent Method
setTwoSideAngleExtent.description: Defines the angle of the revolve to be to applied to both sides of the profile at the specified angles.

parameters.name: angleOne
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle for the first side of the revolution

parameters.name: angleTwo
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle for the second side of the revolution

setTwoSideToExtent.title: RevolveFeatureInput.setTwoSideToExtent Method
setTwoSideToExtent.description: Defines the extents of the revolve to be from the sketch plane to specified faces in both directions. If the matchShape argument is true, the faces to revolve to are extended to fully intersect the revolve.

parameters.name: toEntityOne
parameters.description: The first entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.

parameters.name: toEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.
